assessment.note: Detections driven by SpCond predictions exceeding 10000 μS/cm at coastal/estuarine sites, not by temporal anomaly progression. The model predicts similarly high SpCond for ALL images at these sites (geographic bias, not temporal prediction). Mississippi salinity detection is partially meaningful since the event was indeed a salinity intrusion, but Chesapeake detection is a false positive driven by naturally high estuarine conductance.
assessment.conclusion: Current WaterDroneNet performance (DO R²=0.26, Temp R²=0.44, SpCond R²=0.52) is insufficient for standalone crisis prediction from imagery alone. The model needs more training data and higher accuracy to distinguish normal seasonal variation from genuine pre-event anomalies. SENTINEL Mini is most valuable as a trigger for full SENTINEL stations, not as a standalone crisis detector.
next_steps: Retrain on expanded dataset (7000+ samples vs 1440)
Add temporal context (multi-date sequences instead of single images)
Combine with SENTINEL trigger system for confirmation-based detection
